I am a concerned resident from Memphis, Tennessee. comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: Please see the attached supplemental technical analysis regarding Proposed Rule § 1.45Z-1(f)(6). This analysis addresses the PWA multiplier and labor continuity requirements for high-pathogen, high-hazard, sustainable feedstock operations.

The model ((45ZCF- GREET) created by the Argonne National Laboratory, and employed by the Federal Government, does not fully recognize the total methane emissions reduction achieved from animal feedstock waste. The model applies a lower threshold, (-31 kg CO2e/MMBtu), to manure-based RNG that undervalues the environmental impact. The LifeCycle Analysis (LCA)...

As a producer utilizing swine waste, I strongly urge the Treasury to align the 45ZCF-GREET model with the established lifecycle analysis (LCA) standards used in the California Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS). Key Points for Consideration: * Methane Avoidance Parity: Current 45ZCF-GREET defaults significantly undervalue avoided methane emissions from lagoon-b...

I support Treasury’s move to fold multiple fuel incentives into one, technology-neutral §45Z credit and to permit direct pay/transfer. To ensure the rule works in practice, please consider five aspects of the proposed rule: Registration: Let producers satisfy §4101 and the §6417/6418 pre-filing in a single on-line form; duplicative filings add cost without i...

While the negative integer for horse manure RNG is a welcome start, we request that horse meat farm pipeline-grade gas be fully eligible for the credit without the burden of high-grade ASTM refining. This NPRM regarding the Section 45Z Clean Fuel Production Credit is a well needed step in being able to provide the regulatory certainty needed to scale up domestic clean fuel production. The following comment is submitted in support of the proposed regulations implementing Section 45Z, Clean Fuel Production Credit. The comment provides specific recommendations on issues related to additionality, lifecycle emissions verification, and incentives. The proposed regulations reflect a technically rigorous and policy-relevant approach to lifecycle ...
Awesome informing So helpful unexpectedly
I fully support making the rules more clear and giving taxpayers a better understanding of the procedural rules around this tax credit.

After reading the proposed regulations for the Section 45Z clean fuel production credit, I can see how detailed and technical this process is, especially when it comes to determining emissions rates and eligibility requirements. I think it makes sense that the IRS and Treasury are trying to create clear rules around lifecycle greenhouse gas emissions and reg...

There are two clean fuels that I can thnk of - wind and solar. The idea that coal can be clean is laughable. I am old enough to remember heating with coal in open fireplaces and how filthy it was. And riding in steam trains with specks of black flying through the window if you dared open it. I am a scientist with a doctorate. To ignore the science of climate...

I urge Treasury and IRS to preserve full negative emissions crediting for dairy‑manure‑based RNG under section 45Z. Allowing the 45Z credit to fully reflect the extremely low carbon intensity of dairy RNG is critical to keeping digesters financially viable, supporting struggling dairy farmers, and maximizing methane reductions. Any cap or artificial floor on...

​While the PWA standards are feasible for large industrial energy plants, they place an undue administrative and financial burden on small-scale agricultural producers. I urge Treasury and the IRS to preserve uncapped, or at minimum substantially higher, negative emissions crediting for dairy‑manure–based RNG under section 45Z.
